# Damask

> Self-hosted digital asset management for creative teams that own their data.

Damask is an open-source DAM system you run on your own server. It replaces
cloud DAM services (Bynder, Brandfolder, Canto) for teams that want full data
ownership: all files, all metadata, and all audit history stay on infrastructure
you control.

## Docs

- Docs: /docs/help
- Self-hosting guide: /docs/tech
- API reference: /docs/tech/api

## Core concepts

- **Workspace** — the top-level tenant. One Damask instance can serve multiple
  isolated workspaces.
- **Project** — groups assets by client, campaign, or shoot. Color-coded in
  the sidebar.
- **Folder** — two-level hierarchy inside each project.
- **Asset** — a file (image, video, audio, document, or any binary). Each
  asset tracks its full version history.
- **Version** — a new file uploaded to an existing asset. The previous version
  is preserved; rollback is one click.
- **Variant** — a non-destructive derivative of an asset (resized, transcoded,
  watermarked, background-removed, etc.). The original is never touched.
- **Tag** — workspace-scoped labels. Auto-created on first use.
- **Collection** — a cross-project named set of assets. Membership is additive;
  removing an asset from a collection never deletes the original.
- **Share** — a public link for any asset, project, or collection. No recipient
  account required. Supports password protection, expiry, and client review
  with comments.
- **Ingress source** — an automated ingest pipeline (email, SFTP, WebDAV, S3,
  Google Drive, Canva) that pulls files into Damask on a schedule.
- **Workflow** — an automation graph (trigger → condition → action) that runs
  on asset events or a schedule.
- **Custom field** — user-defined metadata fields per workspace (text, number,
  date, single-select, multi-select). Filterable and full-text searchable.
- **Audit log** — append-only record of every metadata change: who, when, and
  what the previous value was.

## Key capabilities

- Full-text search (FTS5) across filenames, custom text fields, and OCR tracks
- Asset variants: resize, crop, format conversion, watermark, background
  removal, video transcoding, frame capture, audio transcode/normalize
- Three roles: owner, editor, viewer
- OIDC single sign-on (Keycloak, Authelia, Authentik, Okta, Auth0, or any
  standard OIDC provider)
- In-process job queue — no Redis, no external broker required
- SQLite by default; storage layer is swappable (local path or S3-compatible)
- Single Go binary, no external runtime dependencies
- Dark and light modes; keyboard-navigable; WCAG AA

## Tech stack

- Backend: Go + Fiber
- Frontend: SvelteKit + TypeScript
- Database: SQLite with FTS5 (sqlc-generated queries)
- Auth: Paseto v4 tokens
- File storage: local filesystem or S3-compatible (interface-swappable)
- Observability: OpenTelemetry traces + metrics
